plan.action: Verify lib/prd.sh already implements all acceptance criteria: prd_validate checks for valid JSON and non-empty userStories array; prd_next_story filters passes=false stories, sorts by priority ascending, and returns the first; prd_select_next reads from <root>/Input/prd.json; return code 2 signals all-done clean exit.
Verify lib/prd.sh is sourced in clippy.sh (or the appropriate entrypoint) so prd_select_next is available at runtime.
Confirm prd_select_next is called in lib/loop.sh (loop_run_iteration or equivalent) and that a return code of 2 causes the loop to exit cleanly with an informational message (not an error).
Run the existing tests/prd.bats suite to confirm all 11 tests pass without modification.
If any gap is found — e.g. loop does not handle exit code 2, or prd.sh is not sourced — make the minimal targeted fix.

testing_recommendations: Run `bats tests/prd.bats` — all 11 existing tests must pass green.
Add a test in tests/prd.bats (if missing) that confirms prd_next_story returns valid JSON output parseable by jq when a story is selected.
Add an integration-style test in tests/loop.bats (or cli.bats) that confirms the loop exits with status 0 and prints a completion message when prd_select_next returns 2.
Verify shellcheck passes on lib/prd.sh with no errors (typecheck equivalent for bash).
risks: Implementation already exists in lib/prd.sh — risk is that loop.sh does not correctly handle the return-code-2 all-done path, causing an error exit instead of a clean exit.
If prd.sh is not sourced in clippy.sh the functions will be unavailable at runtime despite tests passing.
